# Image Description

This is a vintage trade card or advertisement for Spreckels Rotisserie, a San Francisco establishment from the early 20th century. The card features an ornate golden banner at the top containing the business name and details: "A. Wolff prop. 15th Floor Call Bldg San Francisco." The publisher is credited as Edward H. Mitchell in San Francisco.

The main illustration depicts a dining scene 250 feet above the bay, with two elegantly dressed diners—a man in a dark suit and a woman in a coral-colored dress—seated at a white tablecloth-covered table. They are dining by a large window offering a panoramic view of San Francisco's cityscape, featuring numerous buildings in red and brown tones spreading across hills. On the table between them are a wine bottle, glasses, a floral centerpiece, and dining settings.

To the left of the main scene is a smaller inset image showing the Call Building's distinctive tall tower with a domed top, labeled with "CAFE." At the bottom of the card, descriptive text reads "Ice Cream Parlor - Cafe and Restaurant" and "250 Feet Above the Bay," emphasizing the venue's elevated location. The overall color palette includes soft blues, earth tones, and sepia hues characteristic of early-1900s color printing.
Collection name: Walter Robert and Gail Lynn Kransky collection of Edward H. Mitchell postcards
